exemplar.title: How does Emily Dickinson use the imagery and symbolism of nature to explore issues of female identity and dominant societal norms regarding women in her poetry?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Emily Dickinson use the imagery and symbolism of nature to explore issues of female identity and dominant societal norms regarding women in her poetry?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Clear and sustained focus throughout, with the title, headings and analytical sections consistently sign-posted back to the research question
Research question is sharply defined and tightly linked to the two chosen poems, driving each section of the discussion
Methodology is well-justified, with a coherent combination of close reading, thematic coding and theoretical application
weaknesses: Minor redundancy in restating the research question twice in the introduction
Methodological write-up omits explicit criteria for selecting and evaluating some secondary sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall the work communicates its topic with precision and maintains a consistently appropriate focus (band 5â6). The research question is clearly stated and effectively connected to every analytical section. The methodological approach is complete and informedâjustifying primary text provenance and employing thematic and line-by-line analysesâthough greater clarity on secondaryâsource selection would enhance transparency. These minor limitations sit at the very top of the band, warranting the maximum score of 6.

strengths: Sources are integrated in highly relevant and appropriate ways, with quotations and theoretical arguments reinforcing key analytical points
Subject knowledge of Dickinsonâs poetic form and historical context is coherent and deep, framing the poems effectively
Terminology (e.g. anaphora, catalectic, ecofeminism) is used accurately and consistently throughout
weaknesses: Occasional reliance on paraphrase rather than precise textual quotation
Sporadic over-emphasis on biographical/Victorian context at the expense of deeper textual nuance in places
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates clear, coherent knowledge of both primary texts and relevant theoretical frameworks (band 5â6). Source material is applied in ways that directly inform and support the analysis. Literary and theoretical terminology are used with precision and consistency. Minor lapses in quotation versus paraphrase and occasional contextual over-breadth do not detract from the overall coherence and sophistication, justifying a top-band score of 6.

strengths: Secondary criticism is purposefully selected and consistently tied back to the research question
Analysis of diction, metre and symbolism is deep and well-connected to feminist and ecofeminist arguments
Discussion is logically organised, with a focused, coherent line of argument and acknowledgment of limitations (e.g. two-poem corpus)
weaknesses: Limited evaluation of source authority (e.g. reliance on a non-academic website) and minimal triangulation of perspectives
Counter-arguments and alternative theoretical frameworks are touched on only briefly, reducing argumentative rigor in places
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates excellent relevance and depth of analysis (research quality and depthâââband 10â12) and builds a focused, coherent argument (discussionâââband 10â12). Minor inconsistencies in source evaluation and a lack of sustained counter-argument prevent the very top mark, placing it at 11 of 12.

strengths: Structure is fully appropriate and complete, with all required elements (title page, TOC, numbered sections, bibliography) present
Layout conventions (word count, page numbering, consistent referencing style) are correctly applied and enhance readability
weaknesses: A few minor instances of mid-sentence breaks could be smoothed for flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets the highest descriptor for both structure and layout (band 3â4). All required formatting elements are correctly applied, with clear headings and consistent styling. Minor typographical flow issues do not undermine the overall clarity, justifying the maximum score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly written in interrogative form and tightly linked to two chosen poems, giving it a manageable scope.  Throughout the essay each analytical section is sign-posted back to answering this question, showing effective connection to the ensuing discussion.  Slight unevenness in wording (two near-identical versions of the RQ) keeps it from the very top of the band.

Close readings identify diction, metre, punctuation and symbolism and connect them to feminist themes, showing clear analytical engagement.  Nonetheless, some sections drift into summary or assertion (e.g., broad claims about Victorian men) with limited textual proof, and counter-arguments are scarce.  The depth is good for SL but minor inconsistencies keep it at the top of the 4-6 band rather than the 7-9 band.

Quotations from theoretical writers (Gilbert & Gubar, Plumwood, Taylor-Wiseman, etc.) are integrated into the argument and generally relevant to the points being made.  Some sources are referenced only in passing and occasional paraphrase substitutes for precise textual evidence, but overall application is mostly appropriate and supports the analysis.

Required layout elementsâtitle, word count, section headings, in-text citations and full bibliographyâare present and correctly applied.  Page numbers appear in the table of contents and appear to match the body pages.  Visual balance and formatting are neat, satisfying the highest band for layout.

The student demonstrates solid knowledge of Dickinsonâs historical context, poetic form and feminist/eco-feminist theory, structuring this knowledge coherently around the chosen poems.  Depth is unevenâbiographical and Victorian background sometimes dominate at the expense of textual nuanceâyet the use of sources remains partially effective and clearly shows subject understanding.

The essay follows the expected EE structure with title page, abstract-like introduction, numbered chapters, conclusion, bibliography and accurate table of contents.  Headings and sub-headings aid clarity, and referencing style is consistent, meeting the descriptor for a clearly appropriate and complete structure.

The selection of secondary criticism is purposeful and clearly relevant to the RQ; primary material is focused on two poems with justified rationale.  While scope is narrow, the gathered material is consistently linked to the investigation and demonstrates goodânot merely adequateârelevance.  Limited triangulation and evaluation of source quality prevent entry into the highest band.

The student states the overall focus in the title, restates it in the opening paragraph, and consistently returns to it in section headings and analytical subsections.  The relationship between Dickinsonâs nature imagery and female identity is consistently foregrounded and is demonstrably appropriate to English B Category 3.  Minor over-breadth (Victorian background digressions) prevents a perfect score, but purpose and focus remain clear and sustained.

The discussion is coherent, logically organised and returns to the RQ in the conclusion, demonstrating reasoned argumentation.  Evaluation of limitations (two-poem corpus, theoretical lens constraints) is present but brief.  Some claims remain unchallenged, and alternative readings are not thoroughly weighed, placing the work solidly in the âgood/ effectiveâ range rather than âexcellentâ.

The essay explains a clear procedure (close reading, thematic analysis, application of feminist and ecofeminist theory) and justifies the primary text selection.  It lists a range of supporting secondary sources.  However, only two poems are analysed and little detail is given on how sources were selected or evaluated for reliability, so the methodology, while mostly complete, is not fully convincing or consistently applied.

Literary terminology (iambic trimeter, catalectic, anaphora, symbolism, ecofeminism, etc.) is used accurately and consistently.  Definitions are mostly correct and applied appropriately to the poems.  Minor lapses (capitalisation of theory names, occasional redundancy) do not detract from generally confident and precise conceptual language.

annotations.comment: Including the word count at the start demonstrates careful adherence to IB formatting requirements, ensuring compliance with the word limit rule.
The title clearly communicates the investigationâs focus on nature imagery and female identity, effectively framing the research for an English B Category 3 essay.
The research question is explicit and interrogative, but two near-identical versions appear; consolidate the wording into a single, precise formulation.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly structured, aiding navigation and demonstrating a complete presentation format.
The introduction repeats the research question nearly verbatim in the first paragraph; remove redundancy to maintain focus and coherence.
The Victorian background discussion is informative but somewhat over-broad, diluting the immediate focus on Dickinson; streamline historical context to better support the research question.
The detailed explanation of the âcult of domesticityâ offers good context, but its link to Dickinsonâs poetry is not explicit; connect this background directly to your textual analysis.
The biographical and Romanticism context is coherently organized and demonstrates solid subject knowledge of Dickinsonâs influences.
The methodology outlines close reading and thematic analysis clearly but omits criteria for selecting and evaluating secondary sources; explain your source-selection process.
The plan to integrate line-by-line and thematic analysis is sound, but the steps of thematic coding and identification could be more precisely defined.
The theoretical framework is well-justified, applying both feminist and ecofeminist lenses cohesively to frame the analysis.
Justifying the use of Johnsonâs Complete Poems edition demonstrates attention to primary text provenance, strengthening the methodological rigor.
The initial analysis of âIâm Nobody! Who are you?â offers general reading points but lacks precise feminist theory integration; deepen textual links to your chosen theoretical lens.
Several bibliography entries mix APA and MLA conventions; standardize italics, punctuation, and ordering to one consistent citation style.
The integration of Gilbert and Gubarâs commentary enhances the language analysis and supports the central argument effectively.
Relying on a non-academic source (Symbolopedia) for key symbolism risks undermining research credibility; consider substituting with peer-reviewed criticism.
The transition into the literary-techniques subsection ends mid-thought; complete the sentence for better flow and argument coherence.
Quoting Taylor-Wiseman on nature and gender enriches the ecofeminist discussion, illustrating focused and coherent engagement with theory.
Repeated phrasing of âquiet rebellionâ appears; vary terminology to maintain reader engagement and clarity of argument.
The metrical analysis detailing iambic variation aptly supports the theme of nonconformity, demonstrating precise use of poetic terminology.
A sentence breaks off without resolution in the form analysis; ensure every analytical point is fully articulated for reader clarity.
Scansion notation (marking stresses and feet) would concretely support claims about meter and rhythm, strengthening metrical analysis.
Transcription of âHope is the thing with feathersâ is accurate and well-placed to support the following analysis.
The citation for Lesley Kordecki lacks publisher information and consistent formatting; ensure every reference includes complete details in the chosen style.
Consider alternative interpretive frameworks beyond ecofeminism to acknowledge theoretical limitations and enrich critical depth.
Your discussion of anaphora effectively links form to meaning, demonstrating strong analytical insight into poetic technique.
The nuanced explanation of trochaic opening and catalectic ending in the first line shows precise use of metrical terminology and deep poetic knowledge.
The critical evaluation of essentialist risks in nature imagery offers a focused and coherent argument about the limitations of gendered metaphors.
The discussion of reductive interpretations and their implications for Dickinsonâs critique demonstrates excellent awareness of theoretical nuance.
The bibliography includes both scholarly monographs and informal websites; critically reflect on source authority and consider including more peer-reviewed criticism.
